title: Whats with the band aids
wikitext:
[]: What's with the band-aids?             Quote Post by A.T. Fish » Sat Feb 19, 2011 3:31 pm I feel like this is something I should have deducted from watching the movie, but I have yet to understand what is the meaning of the band-aids that both Rei and Asuka are seen with. I imagine it's some kind of exam that Ritsuko puts them through, but I can't specify the purpose of it. When Shinji asks Rei what they are she says she'll tell him later, this later never comes, and in the elevator scene, when Asuka notices the band-aids in Rei's hands she looks at her own, seemingly sympathizing with Rei at that moment. My question is, does anyone understand why the girls are using those band-aids? Is there some obvious thing I'm missing here?

[]:             Quote Post by Seele00TextOnly » Sat Feb 19, 2011 3:32 pm ...

[]:             Quote Post by Xard » Sat Feb 19, 2011 3:38 pm because it's extremely, extremely moe to show how hard the two dojikko girls devoted themselves to COOKING I can only imagine how many fanboys had heart attacks in Japanese theatres bach then

[]:             Quote Post by A.T. Fish » Sat Feb 19, 2011 3:38 pm It looks so obvious now, I feel dumb.

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Sat Feb 19, 2011 3:41 pm In general, anime girls (or at least significant character anime girls) can't cook -- that was an old cliché even when NGE was being made for TV, and one that continues to the present day. It is supposed to be hilarious. Of course, it also means that yes, officially, Asuka is a cutter.

[]:             Quote Post by Seele00TextOnly » Sat Feb 19, 2011 3:43 pm ...

[]:             Quote Post by esselfortium » Sat Feb 19, 2011 3:54 pm Because to win the protagonist's affections you must be the most domestic ( and have the biggest breasts )

[]:             Quote Post by Xard » Sat Feb 19, 2011 4:11 pm Mr. Tines wrote: In general, anime girls (or at least significant character anime girls) can't cook -- that was an old cliché even when NGE was being made for TV, and one that continues to the present day. It is supposed to be hilarious. Of course, it also means that yes, officially, Asuka is a cutter.

[]:             Quote Post by Ornette » Sat Feb 19, 2011 4:14 pm Seele00TextOnly wrote: Nevermind the fact that they've each been undergoing military combat training for the better part of a decade and the prog knife is a key part of their arsenal; of course they're so unbelievably clutzy and 'moe' that they cut their hands to ribbons.

[]:             Quote Post by AuraTwilight » Sat Feb 19, 2011 4:23 pm Of course, it also means that yes, officially, Asuka is a cutter.

[]:             Quote Post by SaltyJoe » Sat Feb 19, 2011 4:26 pm Seele00TextOnly wrote: Nevermind the fact that they've each been undergoing military combat training for the better part of a decade and the prog knife is a key part of their arsenal

[]:             Quote Post by GasmaskAvenger » Sat Feb 19, 2011 4:57 pm hate to come off as a dick, but this isn't really something that was needed to be asked you see them trying to cook and using knifes, which should translate to "accidental cuts and band aids" as for the cooking subplot, it never bothered me because it's a relatively minor and harmless part of the movie (inb4 the dumb "it's misogynist complaints come in)

[]:             Quote Post by A.T. Fish » Sat Feb 19, 2011 5:11 pm The accidents weren't that obvious to me, specially with Asuka, who in her cooking scene seems to be handling things quite well, and she already has the band-aids in that scene, so I just figured it was something that had happened before and unrelated to the cooking. In the elevator scene, when Asuka looks at both her and Rei's band-aids the tone is such a serious one that I just didn't link it to such a trivial thing as cooking. I see how obvious it is now, but I just didn't make the connection when I watched the movie, hence my questioning.

[]:             Quote Post by Warren Peace » Sat Feb 19, 2011 6:02 pm Asuka's realization that Rei can do something worse than herself (she has more band aids) is what causes Asuka to drop her guard. She had accused Rei of not caring about anything human. The wounds prove that this is not the case, and they have at least that much in common.

[]:             Quote Post by Seele00TextOnly » Sat Feb 19, 2011 6:26 pm ...

[]:             Quote Post by Xard » Sat Feb 19, 2011 6:29 pm Seele00TextOnly wrote: Look, when someone cuts themselves while cutting vegetables, it'll probably just be the fingertips. These band aids are all over their hands, and it's just goofy. Playing up silly anime tropes should've been relegated to the early parts of 1.0 if at all, much like the series. But worse, they made this integral to the plot. The cutting-yourself-more-than-me thing helped lead to Asuka's choice to pilot Eva-03 and decide that if Shinji and the science experiement want eachother, then they can have eachother.

[]: I made this picture for you. Is it glorious?             Quote Post by EvangelionFan » Sat Feb 19, 2011 6:39 pm I acknowledge that the film uses this cooking/cutting/band-aid stuff to show us which of the two girls is (supposedly) working harder for Shinji, and serves a purpose in that it partly convinces Asuka to skip the party and volunteer to test-pilot Unit-03; however I agree with what's been said in that it comes across as a cliché that we're not used to seeing in Evangelion. SPOILER: Show

[]:             Quote Post by KnightmareX13 » Sat Feb 19, 2011 6:54 pm I wonder if there is some sort of product placement going on with the band aids?

[]:             Quote Post by GasmaskAvenger » Sat Feb 19, 2011 6:56 pm Seele00TextOnly wrote: Playing up silly anime tropes should've been relegated to the early parts of 1.0 if at all,, much like the series.

[]:             Quote Post by Defectron » Sat Feb 19, 2011 7:41 pm They're apparently frantically teaching themselves to cook, cutting themselves while chopping vegetables presumably. There's close ups of the kitchen knives, for Rei at least. Of course this is all completely absurd and several of the places that they are cut are preposterous considering the circumstances, but there you go. Such clever clever movies.
